titleStmt.title: K. 184. North interior wall of east gopura I of Prasat Thom, 843 Śaka

licence.p: This work is licenced under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 Unported Licence. To view a copy of the licence, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.

handDesc.p: The lettering is characteristic of the tenth century CE.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_IdListMembers_v01.xml file.

lg.l: (kr̥)tvā śākam aśeṣabhūpatipatiṁ yaṁ hetum(ā)traṁ ha - s

div.p:
[sruk] {3} si po {2} (20) 6 (gvāl) 8 (tai) 20 5 la(p) 6 ... [si] rat 7 si po 5 tai ............ [sru]k vn(ur)
taṅko si 30 10 (I) ........... (tai po -) si A ......................
taṅko si 30 10 (I) ........... (tai po -) si A ......................
.......................... (lvan 3 si rat) ....................
phsaṁ {3} @ [sruk] vaṅ ..................... [ra]t 8 si po 6 tai (po) ............ lva-
n .......... (rat) 2 ......... (rat) I ......... I tai (po) I (phsaṁ) ....... @ (sru)[k] {2} (ṅ) si 40 9 gvāl 10 tai ...... [si] (po)
(2 tai) .......... (si) - 5 ........... (tai lvan) ......... 3 tai rat 2 si (po) .......... [sruk ......]
y ................ g[v]ā[l] .............. (3 si) po 6 (tai po) 5 si A 3 tai .....
si ........ (tai rat) 3 si po 2 tai po 2 phsaṁ 60 2 @ sruk da[ṁ]nap si - (gvāl) - tai 3 ....
si ........ (tai rat) 3 si po 2 tai po 2 phsaṁ 60 2 @ sruk da[ṁ]nap si - (gvāl) - tai 3 ....
..... sruk vnur ca[ṁ]pok si 10 5 gvāl 5 tai 20 lap 3 si rat .......
si lvan 2 gvā(l) - tai lvan 4 lap 2 si rat I tai rat 2 si po 2 tai po 2 si A 2 ....... [sruk] ...(lveṅ) ...
(si) - (gvāl) - tai (20 2 lap) - 3 si rat 10 I tai rat 10 2 si po 10 5 tai po 10 6 [si] (lva)n - (tai lvan) 4 phsaṁ 100 20 10 @
[sruk] {2} y saṅke si (20 2 gvāl) 6 tai 20 3 lap 4 si (ra)t 6 tai rat 10 2 si [po] .......... si lvan 6
....... (si po) ............. [sru]k vaCe ................. 5 si rat 3
tai rat 5 si .............. tai A 5 si lvan 8 (tai) lvan 10 lap 3 si ....... [sruk .... pramā]n vīre-
ndra - vnaṁ sr[uk] ............ 2 tai 10 4 lap I si rat 2 tai ra[t] ............
{2} tra si .......
...

Hommage à Śiva! Celui qui, sans commencement lui-même, est le commencement [le principe] du monde entier, à commencer par le dieu aux quatre visages, qui ... étant sans corps, prend cependant huit corps ...
Le hutabhuj ... les kendra, le samudra ..., relatif à l'astérisme Puṣya ... Vénus, le Soleil avec Mercure ... Br̥haspati, l'horoscope ... Saturne occupe le māna ... les temps efficaces ...

... lui, le maître de tous les rois, que ..., dans l'assemblée par sa fortune ..., ce roi Śrī Jayavarman a donné par dévotion tout ceci qui est la merveille de son royaumeet ce qui s'y trouve de plus précieux ...

The Sanskrit text was first edited by by Auguste Barth and Abel Bergaigne (557-560) with a French translation. It is re-edited by Dominic Goodall in April 2015; with variant readings after Claude Jacques (86-87), and Bourdonneau et al. (nd), in notes. The Khmer text is given after Jacques (90), with variant readings after Bourdonneau et al. (nd), in note.
